"He was very tired and had to stop to rest sometimes." "No one made him hurry now, everything was his choice." "When he finished turning, he started to move forward." "He was surprised by how far his room was. He did not know how he got there before when he was weak and did not see it." "He tried to crawl very fast. He did not hear his family say anything to stop him." "He did not look back until he got to the door." "He didn't turn all the way because his neck hurt. But he saw that nothing changed behind him. Only his sister stood up." In his last look, he saw his mother was now fully asleep. "He went into his room and quickly shut, bolted, and locked the door." "The loud noise behind Gregor scared him and made his small legs give out." "His sister was the one who was in a big hurry." "She was standing there and waiting. Then, she moved fast. Gregor did not hear her. She turned the key in the lock. She said to her parents, 'At last!'" "." "What do we do now?" "Gregor asked himself something when he looked around in the dark." "He soon found out that he could not move at all." "He was not surprised. He thought it was strange to walk with his thin, small legs before." "He also felt quite comfortable." "His whole body hurt, but the pain was slowly going away and would soon be gone." "He could not really feel the bad apple on his back or the sore area around it, which was all covered in white dust." "He remembered his family with feelings and love." "If he could, he felt he needed to leave more than his sister." "He stayed calm and quiet until he heard the big clock say it was three in the morning." "He saw it slowly start to get light outside the window too." Then, he did not want it, but his head fell down. His last breath came out softly from his nose. The cleaner came early in the morning. She often slammed the doors even when asked not to. This woke everyone up. They knew she was there and could not sleep well. She looked at Gregor as she always did. At first, she saw nothing strange. "She thought he was not moving on purpose, acting like a victim. She believed he knew everything." "She had a long broom in her hand. She tried to tickle Gregor with it from the door." "When she did not win, she tried to bother him. She pushed him a bit. But when she could push him and he did not stop her, she started to care." "She knew what happened. She opened her eyes big and made a sound. But she did not wait. She opened the bedroom doors fast and yelled into the dark rooms: Come and see this, it is dead, just lying there, very dead!" "Mr." "and Mrs." "Samsa sat up in bed. He was shocked by the cleaner. He had to try hard to understand her words." "But then, they quickly got out of bed, each from their own side." "Mr." "Samsa put the blanket on his shoulders, Mrs." "Samsa came out in her nightdress. That's how they went into Gregor's room." "They walked and opened the door to the room. Grete was sleeping there after three men came to live. She was wearing all her clothes like she did not sleep at all. Her face was very pale and it looked like she did not sleep." "Dead?" ", asked Mrs." "Samsa looked at the cleaning lady. She could have checked herself and known it without looking." "I said that," the cleaner said. She pushed Gregor's body with the broom again. This made him move across the floor. "Mrs." "Samsa tried to stop the broom, but she did not do it." "Now, Mr. said," "Samsa, let's say thank you to God for this." "He made a cross sign on himself, and the three women did the same." Grete, looking at the dead body, said: "See how thin he was." "He did not eat for a long time." "The food came out the same way it went in." "Gregor's body was very dry and flat. They did not see it before, but now he did not stand on his small legs. He did not do anything to make them look away." "Grete, come with us here for a bit," said Mrs. "Samsa smiled sadly and Grete went with her parents to the bedroom. But she looked back at the body." The cleaner closed the door and opened the window a lot. "Even though it was early morning, the air felt a bit warm." "It was the end of March already." The three men left their room and looked for their breakfasts. They were surprised because no one remembered them. "Where is our morning food?" "The man in the middle asked the cleaner in an angry way." "She put her finger on her lips. She made a quiet sign to the men. She showed they can come into Gregor's room." "They did it and stood near Gregor's dead body. Their hands were in their old coat pockets." The room was now very bright. Then the bedroom door opened and Mr. "Samsa came in his work clothes. His wife was on one side and his daughter on the other." "They all cried a bit. Sometimes, Grete put her face on her father's arm." "Go away from my house." "Now!" ", said the man." "Samsa points to the door and does not let the women leave him." "What do you mean?" ", asked the man in the middle of the three, a bit confused, and he smiled nicely." The other two put their hands behind their backs. They kept rubbing them together, happy for a big fight they could win. "I said what I mean," Mr. answered. "Samsa and his two friends walked straight to the man." At first, he just stood there. He looked at the ground like he was thinking hard. "Okay, we will go then," he said, and looked at Mr. "Samsa felt shy all of a sudden and wanted to ask Mr. for permission again." "Samsa made a choice." "Mr." "Samsa just opened his eyes big and nodded to him a few times." "Right away, the man walked fast into the front hall. His two friends had stopped moving their hands. They were listening to the talk." "Now they jumped off after their friend, scared that Mr." "Samsa may walk to the hall before them and stop talking to their boss." "When they got there, all three took their hats and sticks. They said nothing, bowed, and left." "Mr." "Samsa and the two women went out to the landing. But they did not think the men were bad. They looked over the landing. They saw the three men go down the steps slowly but without stopping." "When they went around each floor, they were gone and came back after a bit. The more they went down, the less the Samsa family cared about them. A boy from the butcher shop walked by them. He held a tray on his head and stood tall. He was closer to them than Mr. Samsa." "Samsa and the women left the landing. They went back into the flat and felt better." "They chose to relax and walk that day. They needed a break from work and they really needed it." "So they sat at the table and wrote three sorry letters, sir." "Samsa talks to his bosses, Mrs." "Samsa talks to her worker and Grete talks to her boss." "The cleaner came in when they were writing. She told them she was leaving because she finished her morning work." At first, they just said yes without looking up from their writing. But when the cleaner did not leave, they looked up and felt annoyed. "Well?" ", Mr. asked." "Samsa." The cleaning lady stood at the door. She was smiling like she had great news. But, she would only tell if asked. "The small feather on her hat stood up. It annoyed Mr." "Samsa moved slowly in all ways while she worked for them." "What do you want then?" ", asked Mrs." "The cleaner had a lot of respect for Samsa." "Yes," she said, and laughed so much she couldn't talk right away. "Don't worry about that thing inside. You don't need to think about how to remove it." "That is all fixed now." "Mrs." "Samsa and Grete looked at their letters. They wanted to keep writing." "Samsa saw that the cleaner wanted to talk about everything. But he showed with his hand that she should not." "So, she could not tell them everything. She remembered she was in a rush. She was upset and said 'Goodbye then, everyone'. She turned fast and left. She closed the door hard when she left." "Tonight she loses her job," said Mr. "Samsa got no answer from his wife or daughter. The cleaning lady had broken the quiet they just got." "They stood up and walked to the window. There, they stayed with their arms around each other." "Mr." "Samsa turned in his chair to see them and sat watching for some time." Then he shouted: Come here, then. "Let's not think about the old things, okay." "Come and give me some time." "The two women did what he said right away. They went to him, kissed him, and hugged him. Then they finished their letters fast." "After that, the three of them left the flat together. They had not done this for months. They took the tram to the country outside the town." "They were alone in the tram and it was full of warm sunlight." They sat back in their seats and talked about their jobs. They saw that their jobs were not bad. Before, they never talked about their work. But all three had good jobs. These jobs could be better in the future. "The best change for now would be to move house. They needed a smaller, cheaper flat. Gregor picked the old one. They wanted a new one in a better place and more useful." "Grete was getting more active all the time." "They had been worried and her face was pale. But while they talked, Mr." "and Mrs." Samsa saw that their daughter was becoming a strong and pretty young girl. "They got more quiet." "Just by looking at each other, they knew it was time to find a good man for her." "And, like their new dreams and good plans were right, when they got to the place, Grete was the first to stand up and move her young body."
